game_description: Drop discs to connect four in a row before your opponent, 2 players.
filter_1_label: Did you go first this game?

strategies.strategy_detailed: Connect 4 is solved — first player wins with perfect play. The center column (column 4) is the only correct opening move; it participates in more four-in-a-row lines than any other column. Any other first move is a provably losing choice against optimal play.
strategy_stealth: Connect 4 solved: P1 wins with perfect play
Column 4 = only correct first move
Non-center opening = provable loss

strategies.strategy_detailed: Connect 4 is solved: second player cannot win against perfect play — draw is P2's best achievable outcome. Against center-opening opponent, play column 3 or 5 to contest the diagonal. Never play corners or extreme edges on move 2.
strategy_stealth: P2 cannot win against perfect play
Draw is P2's best achievable result
Counter center: play col 3 or 5

strategies.strategy_detailed: Build threats in two directions simultaneously — horizontal and diagonal from the same disc. An opponent who must block one lets you extend the other. Three in a row with both ends open is an immediate forced win — opponent can only block one end.
strategy_stealth: Build horizontal + diagonal together
Three open-ended in a row = forced win
Two threats = opponent can block one

strategies.strategy_detailed: Second player cannot win against perfect play — a draw is P2's ceiling. Every disc must both threaten your four AND block opponent's four. Pure blocking without counter-threat cedes tempo to P1 and accelerates the forced loss.
strategy_stealth: P2 ceiling = draw, not a win
Every move: threaten AND block
Pure blocking = cede tempo, accelerate loss

strategies.condition: Opponent must respond to your threat
strategies.strategy_detailed: A forced response (opponent must block or lose) lets you build elsewhere. Chain forced responses: after they block, create a new threat in a different area. Three consecutive forced responses often result in a position where you have two simultaneous winning moves.
strategy_stealth: Chain forced blocks in different areas
3 chains = 2 simultaneous win threats
Forced moves = free development elsewhere

strategies.strategy_detailed: The first player wins with threats on odd rows; second player wins on even rows. Plan your winning four-in-a-row to land on the right parity row for your position. A threat on row 3 (odd) benefits the first player; row 4 (even) benefits second. Count your planned winning row.
strategy_stealth: First player wins on odd rows (1,3,5)
Second player wins on even rows (2,4,6)
Count your winning row parity

strategies.condition: Board filling with no winner yet
strategies.strategy_detailed: On a filling board, zugzwang (any move you make loses) becomes a real threat. Avoid building columns where your own disc creates a winning row for the opponent above it. Before each move, check: does this disc set up opponent's winning position one row above?
strategy_stealth: Check: does this disc set up their win above?
Full board zugzwang loses instantly
Avoid columns with opponent win above

strategies.strategy_detailed: Two simultaneous threats (a 'double threat') guarantee a win — opponent can block only one. Create a double threat by building a single disc that completes two different potential four-in-a-rows. If your opponent cannot block both, play the double threat move immediately.
strategy_stealth: Double threat = guaranteed win
One disc completing two different fours
Play it the moment it appears

strategies.condition: Board nearly full, you hold tempo
strategies.strategy_detailed: With 6-8 slots remaining and tempo advantage, count the forced sequence. If your forced win requires opponent to fill a specific column in a specific order, verify they have no alternative that blocks your win along the way. Calculate 3 moves deep before committing.
strategy_stealth: Count forced sequence 3 moves deep
Verify opponent has no blocking alternative
Calculate before committing — not by feel

strategies.strategy_detailed: When first player has tempo advantage and a forced win line, a draw (full board, no winner) is your best achievable outcome. Force the board toward columns that deny their winning parity. A draw is a victory for the second player in a theoretically losing position.
strategy_stealth: Draw = victory for second player
Force board to deny their parity row
Full board with no winner = success
